titleStmt.title: SII 1.155: original edition by Eugen Hultzsch
PART IV. ADDENDA. No. 155. AN INSCRIPTION OF KULOTTUб№„GA-CHOбё¶A AT CHIDAMBARAM.

summary.p: The subjoined Grantha inscription is engraved on the outside of the east wall of the innermost prДЃkДЃra of the great temple at Chidambaram in the South Arcot District. It consists of two verses in the SragdharДЃ metre, each of which eulogises the victories of Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a over the five PДЃб№‡бёЌyas. The first verse further states, that the king burnt the fort of KorgДЃra (KorgДЃra-durga) and defeated the Keraбё·as. KorgДЃra is probably a Sanskritised form of Koб№џkai in the Tinnevelly District, the ancient capital of the PДЃб№‡бёЌyas.For references on Koб№џkai, see Mr. Sewell's Lists of Antiquities, Vol. I, p. 312. The second verse records, that Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a placed a pillar of victory on the SahyДЃdri mountain, i.e., the Western GhДЃб№­s. This he must have done after his conquest of the Keraбё·as, which is mentioned in the first verse.

According to a grant published by Mr. Fleet,Ind. Ant. Vol. XIV, p. 55. Kulottuб№…ga-ChoбёЌa-deva was the name of two of the Eastern Chalukyan successors of the Choбё·a kings. Of the first of these, who was also called RДЃjendra-ChoбёЌa and ruled from Ељaka 985 to 1034, the ChellЕ«r grant reports that he conquered the Kerala and PДЃб№‡бёЌya countries.See No. 39, verse 10, p. 59, above. From an unpublished Chidambaram inscriptionSee paragraph 5 of my Progress Report for February, March and April 1888, Madras G.O., 27th July 1888, No. 745, Public. it appears, that the surname Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·adeva was also borne by the maternal grandfather of the last-mentioned king, the Choбё·a king RДЃjendra-Choбё·a-deva, among whose conquests we find both the Keraбё·a and PДЃб№‡бёЌya countries.See p. 97, above. Consequently, it is impossible to say to which Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a the subjoined inscription has to be referred.This remark applies also to the various kings of the same name, who are mentioned in other inscriptions of this volume. These are:вЂ”Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a-deva in No. 86; Ko-RДЃjakesarivarman, alias Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a-deva in Nos. 89, 96 and 130; and Koб№‰eri Meб№‰-koб№‡бёЌa Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a-deva in No. 132.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti Е›rД« . pДЃб№‡бёЌyДЃndaб№‡бёЌena jitvДЃ pracuraЕ›aramucДЃ paГ±ca paГ±cДЃnanaЕ›rД«бёҐ dagdhvДЃ korggДЃradurggantб№›iб№‡amiva sa yathДЃ
khДЃб№‡бёЌavamВ· pДЃб№‡бёЌusЕ«nuбёҐ . piб№Јб№­vДЃ tatВ· keraбё·ДЃnДЃm balamatibahaбё·am Е›rД«kulottuб№Ѓgacoбё·aЕ›cakre Е›akrapratДЃpastribhuvanavijayastamВ·bhamamВ·bhodhitД«re . 1
puб№‡ye saб№ЃhyДЃdriЕ›б№›iб№Ѓge tribhuvanavijayastamВ·bhamamВ·bhodhipДЃre svacchandamВ· pДЃrasД«nДЃntaruб№‡ayuvatibhirggД«yate yasya kД«rttiбёҐ .
sa Е›rД«mДЃnastaЕ›atruбёҐ prabalabalabharaiбёҐ paГ±ca pДЃб№‡бёЌyДЃnvijitya kб№ЈubhyatВ· kб№ЈmДЃpДЃlacakram savidhikamakarocchrД«kulottuб№Ѓgacoбё·aбёҐ . 2

app.note: Е›atruбёҐ seems to be corrected from Е›astraбёҐ.

div.p: Hail! Prosperity! (Verse 1.) Having defeated the five PДЃб№‡бёЌyas by an army, which discharged numerous arrows, having burnt, like straw, the fort of KorgДЃra, just as (Arjuna) the son of PДЃб№‡бёЌu burnt the KhДЃб№‡бёЌava (forest),Compare No. 39, verse 3, p. 57, above. and having crushed the extremely dense army of the Keraбё·as,вЂ”the illustrious Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a, who resembled Siva in splendour and Indra in might, placed a pillar (commemorative of his) conquest of the three worlds on the shore of the ocean.

(Verse 2.) (Having placedOne would expect a gerund conveying this meaning instead of ambodhipДЃre, which seems to have crept into the text through the influence of ambodhitД«re in verse 1.) a pillar (commemorative of his) conquest of the three worlds on the sacred peak of the SahyДЃdri (mountain), and having defeated the five PДЃб№‡бёЌyas by masses of powerful armies,вЂ”the illustrious Kulottuб№…ga-Choбё·a, whose fame is voluntarily sung by the tender women of the PДЃrasis,The composer of the inscription mentions the Persians (Tamil pДЃraci, Sanskrit pДЃrasД«ka) merely as the representatives of the most distant nations which were known to him. and who has driven away his enemies, made the trembling crowd of kings subject to his orders.Savidhika seems to be used in the sense of ДЃjГ±ДЃvidheya.
Digital edition of SII 1.155 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis
